name: data-pipeline-etl
description: Extract data from a source URL (JSON array), transform it, and load it into a database table. Optionally injects a Bearer token from a stored secret for authenticated endpoints.

SOURCE_URL.description: URL to fetch source data from. Must return a JSON array of objects.

TARGET_TABLE.description: Target database table name (alphanumeric + underscores only). The table must already exist with columns: remote_id, name, email, company.

API_TOKEN_SECRET.description: Optional: key_path of a secret containing the Bearer token for authenticated source endpoints (e.g. 'api/source-token'). Leave empty for public URLs.

setup_instructions.title: Prepare your database table
setup_instructions.detail: Create the target table before running. Example: CREATE TABLE etl_records (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, remote_id VARCHAR(255), name VARCHAR(255), email VARCHAR(255), company VARCHAR(255));

setup_instructions.title: Store API token (if needed)
setup_instructions.detail: If SOURCE_URL requires authentication, store the token with set_secret and reference its key_path in API_TOKEN_SECRET. Example: key_path='api/source-token'.

setup_instructions.title: Verify source URL
setup_instructions.detail: The SOURCE_URL must return a JSON array of objects with fields: id, name, email, company (nested). Test with curl before deploying.
